name: Course Overview
description: Course path and current Mini LM development status.

lessons.name: Mini Language Model Course
lessons.description: Overview: dataset, tokenizer, baseline LM, neural LM, evaluation, generation, and deployment demo.
lessons.template:
# This overview lesson does not require code.
print("Welcome to the Mini Language Model Course")

name: Lab 01 - Dataset Preparation
description: Prepare clean train, validation, and test text files for language model training.

lessons.name: Lab 01.1: What Is a Text Dataset?
lessons.description: Inspect a short text dataset and count characters, lines, and unique characters.
lessons.template:
text = "robot moves forward\nrobot turns left\nmodel learns text\n"

print(text)
print("Characters:", len(text))
print("Lines:", len(text.splitlines()))
print("Unique characters:", sorted(set(text)))

lessons.name: Lab 01.2: Reading Raw Text
lessons.description: Create and read a raw UTF-8 text file with pathlib.
lessons.template:
from pathlib import Path

raw_text = "robot moves forward\nrobot turns left\nmodel learns text\n"
raw_path = Path("raw_text.txt")
raw_path.write_text(raw_text, encoding="utf-8")

# TODO: read raw_path as UTF-8 text
text = ""

print("Raw characters:", len(text))
print("Preview:", text[:80])

lessons.name: Lab 01.3: Cleaning Text
lessons.description: Normalize newlines, trim trailing spaces, and keep one final newline.
lessons.template:
raw_text = "\nrobot moves forward  \r\nrobot turns left\r\nmodel learns text  \n\n"


def clean_text(text):
    # TODO: normalize Windows newlines to \n
    # TODO: remove trailing spaces on each line
    # TODO: remove empty lines at the beginning and end
    # TODO: return cleaned text with one final newline
    return text


cleaned = clean_text(raw_text)
print("Clean characters:", len(cleaned))
print("Clean lines:", len(cleaned.splitlines()))
print(cleaned)

lessons.name: Lab 01.4: Splitting Train, Validation, and Test
lessons.description: Split cleaned text into train, validation, and test strings.
lessons.template:
text = ("robot moves forward. robot turns left. model learns text.\n" * 20)


def split_text(text, train_ratio=0.8, val_ratio=0.1):
    # TODO: calculate split indexes
    train_text = ""
    val_text = ""
    test_text = ""
    return train_text, val_text, test_text


train_text, val_text, test_text = split_text(text)
print("Train characters:", len(train_text))
print("Validation characters:", len(val_text))
print("Test characters:", len(test_text))

lessons.name: Lab 01.5: Dataset Statistics
lessons.description: Build stats.json fields: num_chars, num_lines, and vocab_chars.

lessons.name: Lab 01.6: Writing Dataset Artifacts
lessons.description: Write train.txt, val.txt, test.txt, stats.json, and dataset_card.md.
lessons.template:
from pathlib import Path
import json

train_text = "robot moves forward\n" * 10
val_text = "robot turns left\n" * 2
test_text = "model learns text\n" * 2
stats = {
    "num_chars": len(train_text + val_text + test_text),
    "num_lines": len((train_text + val_text + test_text).splitlines()),
    "vocab_chars": sorted(set(train_text + val_text + test_text)),
}

# TODO: write train.txt, val.txt, test.txt, stats.json, dataset_card.md

for filename in ["train.txt", "val.txt", "test.txt", "stats.json", "dataset_card.md"]:
    print("Wrote:", filename)

lessons.name: Lab 01.7: Final Dataset Submission
lessons.description: Create the complete dataset preparation submission and required artifacts.
lessons.template:
from pathlib import Path
import json

RAW_TEXT = """
robot moves forward and reads sensors.
robot turns left and follows a line.
small language models learn from text.
python code prepares clean datasets.
""" * 30


def clean_text(text):
    text = text.replace("\r\n", "\n").replace("\r", "\n")
    lines = [line.rstrip() for line in text.split("\n")]
    cleaned = "\n".join(lines).strip()
    return cleaned + "\n"


def split_text(text, train_ratio=0.8, val_ratio=0.1):
    train_end = int(len(text) * train_ratio)
    val_end = train_end + int(len(text) * val_ratio)
    return text[:train_end], text[train_end:val_end], text[val_end:]


def build_stats(text):
    return {
        "num_chars": len(text),
        "num_lines": len(text.splitlines()),
        "vocab_chars": sorted(set(text)),
    }


def main():
    text = clean_text(RAW_TEXT)
    train_text, val_text, test_text = split_text(text)
    stats = build_stats(text)

    Path("train.txt").write_text(train_text, encoding="utf-8")
    Path("val.txt").write_text(val_text, encoding="utf-8")
    Path("test.txt").write_text(test_text, encoding="utf-8")
    Path("stats.json").write_text(json.dumps(stats, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2), encoding="utf-8")
    Path("dataset_card.md").write_text(
        "# Dataset Card\n\nSynthetic local text dataset for Mini LM course checks.\n",
        encoding="utf-8",
    )
    print("Dataset artifacts written")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

name: Lab 02 - Character Tokenizer
description: Convert text to character token ids and decode ids back to text.

lessons.name: Lab 02: Character Tokenizer
lessons.description: Implement encode/decode for a character tokenizer.

name: Lab 03 - Bigram Character Language Model
description: Build and evaluate a count-based next-character baseline model.

lessons.name: Lab 03.1: What Is a Character Language Model?
lessons.description: See text as neighboring character pairs for next-character prediction.
lessons.template:
text = "hello"

# Print every neighboring character pair.
for current_char, next_char in zip(text, text[1:]):
    print(current_char, "->", next_char)

# Try changing the text and run again.

lessons.description: Read train.txt and val.txt from the checker dataset directory.
lessons.template:

val_text = (DATASET_DIR / "val.txt").read_text(encoding="utf-8")

print("Train characters:", len(train_text))
print("Validation characters:", len(val_text))
print("First 200 training characters:")
print(train_text[:200])

lessons.name: Lab 03.6: Validation Loss and Perplexity
lessons.description: Evaluate the bigram model with negative log likelihood and perplexity.
lessons.template:
from pathlib import Path
import math
import os

DATASETS_DIR = Path(os.environ.get("DATASETS_DIR", "/datasets"))
DATASET_DIR = DATASETS_DIR / "toy_chars_100kb"
train_text = (DATASET_DIR / "train.txt").read_text(encoding="utf-8")
val_text = (DATASET_DIR / "val.txt").read_text(encoding="utf-8")


def build_vocab(text):
    vocab = sorted(set(text))
    char_to_id = {char: index for index, char in enumerate(vocab)}
    return vocab, char_to_id


def create_empty_counts(vocab_size):
    return [[0 for _ in range(vocab_size)] for _ in range(vocab_size)]


def count_bigrams(text, char_to_id, vocab_size):
    counts = create_empty_counts(vocab_size)
    for current_char, next_char in zip(text, text[1:]):
        counts[char_to_id[current_char]][char_to_id[next_char]] += 1
    return counts


def row_probabilities(row):
    # TODO: implement add-one smoothing
    return []


def compute_val_loss(text, counts, char_to_id):
    losses = []

    # TODO: compute -math.log(probability) for each known validation pair

    return sum(losses) / len(losses)


vocab, char_to_id = build_vocab(train_text)
counts = count_bigrams(train_text, char_to_id, len(vocab))
val_loss = compute_val_loss(val_text, counts, char_to_id)
perplexity = math.exp(val_loss)

print("Validation loss:", val_loss)
print("Perplexity:", perplexity)

    return [(count + 1) / total for count in row]

lessons.name: Lab 03.8: Final Bigram Submission
lessons.description: Assemble train_bigram.py and save all required artifacts for the checker.
lessons.template:
from pathlib import Path
import json
import math
import os
import random

DATASETS_DIR = Path(os.environ.get("DATASETS_DIR", "/datasets"))
DATASET_DIR = DATASETS_DIR / "toy_chars_100kb"
TRAIN_PATH = DATASET_DIR / "train.txt"
VAL_PATH = DATASET_DIR / "val.txt"


def build_vocab(text):
    vocab = sorted(set(text))
    char_to_id = {char: index for index, char in enumerate(vocab)}
    return vocab, char_to_id


def create_empty_counts(vocab_size):
    return [[0 for _ in range(vocab_size)] for _ in range(vocab_size)]


def count_bigrams(text, char_to_id, vocab_size):
    counts = create_empty_counts(vocab_size)
    for current_char, next_char in zip(text, text[1:]):
        current_id = char_to_id[current_char]
        next_id = char_to_id[next_char]
        counts[current_id][next_id] += 1
    return counts


def row_probabilities(row):
    # TODO: implement add-one smoothing
    return []


def compute_val_loss(text, counts, char_to_id):
    losses = []

    # TODO: compute validation negative log likelihood

    return sum(losses) / len(losses)


def sample_next_char(row, vocab):
    probabilities = row_probabilities(row)
    return random.choices(vocab, weights=probabilities, k=1)[0]


def generate(prompt, counts, vocab, char_to_id, max_new_chars=160):
    if not prompt:
        prompt = random.choice(vocab)

    output = prompt
    current_char = output[-1]

    for _ in range(max_new_chars):
        if current_char not in char_to_id:
            current_char = random.choice(vocab)
        else:
            current_id = char_to_id[current_char]
            current_char = sample_next_char(counts[current_id], vocab)
        output += current_char

    return output


def main():
    random.seed(42)

    train_text = TRAIN_PATH.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
    val_text = VAL_PATH.read_text(encoding="utf-8")

    vocab, char_to_id = build_vocab(train_text)
    counts = count_bigrams(train_text, char_to_id, len(vocab))
    val_loss = compute_val_loss(val_text, counts, char_to_id)
    perplexity = math.exp(val_loss)

    samples = [
        {"prompt": "t", "generated_text": generate("t", counts, vocab, char_to_id)},
        {"prompt": "r", "generated_text": generate("r", counts, vocab, char_to_id)},
        {"prompt": "\n", "generated_text": generate("\n", counts, vocab, char_to_id)}
    ]

    Path("bigram_counts.json").write_text(
        json.dumps(
            {"vocab": vocab, "char_to_id": char_to_id, "counts": counts},
            ensure_ascii=False,
            indent=2
        ),
        encoding="utf-8"
    )

    Path("metrics.json").write_text(
        json.dumps(
            {
                "vocab_size": len(vocab),
                "train_chars": len(train_text),
                "val_chars": len(val_text),
                "val_loss": val_loss,
                "perplexity": perplexity
            },
            indent=2
        ),
        encoding="utf-8"
    )

    Path("samples.json").write_text(
        json.dumps({"samples": samples}, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2),
        encoding="utf-8"
    )

    print(f"trained bigram model with vocab_size={len(vocab)} val_loss={val_loss:.4f}")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

parser = argparse.ArgumentParser()
parser.add_argument("--prompt", default="the robot")
parser.add_argument("--max-new-tokens", type=int, default=100)
parser.add_argument("--temperature", type=float, default=0.8)
args = parser.parse_args()

print("Generation starter")
print("Prompt:", args.prompt)
